codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of the Vitest test framework. All 6 expected behaviors are met: vitest and @vitest/coverage-v8 are in devDependencies, vitest.config.ts exists with a clean Phaser module alias mock, src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts contains 41 well-structured tests across all 5 required systems (CombatSystem 14 tests, PathfindingSystem 5 tests, LootSystem 5 tests, InventorySystem 8 tests, MapGenerator 6 tests, plus 3 skill scaling helpers), npm run test works, npx vitest run exits 0, and coverage includes all 5 core systems. The Phaser mock is minimal and well-scoped (only EventEmitter). Test helpers are clean and reusable. Statistical approaches for randomized mechanics are appropriate.

issues.description: LootSystem tests for generateEquipment use `if (item)` guards around assertions. If generateEquipment returns null/undefined, the test silently passes without verifying anything. Consider using `expect(item).toBeDefined()` before the conditional block or using `assert(item)` to ensure the test actually exercises the code path.

Coverage `include` list is hardcoded to 5 specific system files. New systems added by future features (e.g., StatusEffectSystem, AchievementSystem) won't appear in coverage unless manually added. Consider using a glob pattern like 'src/systems/*.ts' or documenting that new systems need to be added to the coverage config.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The systems-worker skill says 'Write tests FIRST (red)' (step 3) but the worker noted this doesn't apply to test infrastructure setup features where you're bootstrapping the framework itself. The skill's procedure is correct for normal feature work but not for one-time setup tasks. The worker's deviation was justified.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The systems-worker skill says Write tests FIRST (red) but for a test infrastructure setup feature, writing the config and then the tests simultaneously makes more sense since there's nothing to make red first.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The coverage include list in vitest.config.ts is hardcoded. As new systems are added by other workers, they won't appear in coverage unless someone updates this config. AGENTS.md doesn't mention this. Consider documenting that vitest.config.ts coverage.include must be updated when new testable systems are added.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: vitest.config.ts lines 17-22 — only 5 files listed. Later features like StatusEffectSystem and SetBonusEffects already have their own test files but won't appear in coverage reports.

summary: The vitest-setup feature is a clean, well-implemented pass. All 6 expected behaviors are fully satisfied: packages installed, config created with Phaser mocking, 41 tests across 5 systems, npm run test script works, vitest run exits 0, coverage covers all 5 core systems. The Phaser mock approach (module alias to a lightweight EventEmitter stub) is elegant and minimal. Two non-blocking issues noted: (1) some LootSystem tests silently pass if generateEquipment returns null, (2) hardcoded coverage include list won't auto-expand for new systems. Neither issue blocks the feature's acceptance.
